intended_use: The ScreenPoint TransparaTM system is intended for use as a concurrent reading aid for physicians interpreting screening mammograms, to identify regions suspicious for breast cancer and assess their l
intended_use_full: The ScreenPoint TransparaTM system is intended for use as a concurrent reading aid for physicians interpreting screening mammograms, to identify regions suspicious for breast cancer and assess their likelihood of malignancy. Output of the device includes marks placed on suspicious soft tissue lesions and suspicious calcifications; region‐based scores, displayed upon the physician's query, indicating the likelihood that cancer is present in specific regions; and an overall score indicating the likelihood that cancer is present on the mammogram. Patient management decisions should not be made solely on the basis of analysis by Transpara™.
target_condition: Breast cancer detection in screening mammography
ground_truth: Biopsy-proven examples of breast cancer and benign lesions (for algorithm training); retrospectively collected mammograms with known cancer status (for clinical study)
other_metrics: For calcifications: sensitivity 94.1% (90.6-97.1) with false positive rate 0.23 (0.22-0.25) marks per image. For soft tissue lesions: sensitivity 72.0% (67.4-76.5) with false positive rate 0.033 (0.028-0.037) marks per image. Clinical study: average AUC increased from 0.866 (unaided) to 0.886 (with Transpara™), +0.020 (95% CI = 0.010 – 0.030, P=0.0019). For soft tissue lesions alone: AUC increased from 0.886 to 0.902 (+0.016). For calcifications alone: AUC increased from 0.878 to 0.898 (+0.020). Standalone Transpara™ AUC=0.887.
